purpose: This commit addresses a potential use-after-free (UAF) issue in the Bluetooth hci_sync code by checking the HCI_UNREGISTER flag before queuing commands.
Removes an unnecessary include statement in n_hdlc.c, simplifying the include structure and reducing redundancy.
Updates documentation to reflect the addition of an 'N/A' metric for store operations in perf reports.
Fixes issues with vma lock handling during splitting and unmapping in hugetlb, addressing race conditions and ensuring proper lock management.
This commit adds AVDD and MICVDD supply definitions to the dt-bindings for the rt5682s codec, improving documentation.
The commit addresses a resource leak in the zorro7xx SCSI driver's remove function by adding an iounmap() call to properly release resources.
The commit removes an unused work field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx, which improves code cleanliness and maintainability.
The commit changes the type of a struct member from unsigned to signed to prevent static checker warnings when assigning -1. This avoids potential issues with code analysis tools.
This commit sorts the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS configuration in a RISC-V defconfig file to match savedefconfig's order. It does not change functionality but improves code organization.
Introduces a helper function to check if the chip's FIFOs are in CAN-FD mode, replacing an open-coded check.
The commit modifies selftests to skip RTC-related tests when the RTC hardware is not present, preventing unnecessary test failures.
The commit removes an unnecessary variable assignment that was causing a clang warning. It cleans up redundant code without affecting functionality.

The commit decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks in a kernel configured with CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE=y. This change prevents the unnecessary inclusion of RCU Tasks when rcuscale is built, reducing kernel size and complexity.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase 'be split' in the spte.h file within the KVM x86 MMU code.
Reverts a previous change that caused issues with clock table population, preventing underflow.
The commit adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC Mali-G31 GPU in device tree bindings, enabling proper hardware recognition.
Renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for naming consistency in the DW PCIe core driver.
Fixes a clang format warning by correcting the format specifier to match the promoted integer type, improving code correctness and adherence to standards.
Fixes a bug in MIPS jump_label by casting upper bound to long for signed comparison, preventing negative offsets from triggering warnings.
Removes an unnecessary case from SetHwReg8188EU() by directly calling the function it references. Part of a larger effort to eliminate the hal layer.
The commit addresses sparse warnings by declaring non-global symbols as static in arm64 code. This improves code quality and prevents potential linking issues.
The commit corrects device tree bindings by removing incorrect interrupt flags for MDSS nodes, ensuring proper interrupt handling.
The commit modifies memory allocation in struct ctrl_ref to prepare for dynamic array size adjustments by drivers.
Adds support for new SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, SM8450) and associated PMICs in the socinfo driver to enhance hardware compatibility.
This commit updates the rtl8xxxu wifi driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (versions A-P) instead of only up to E. It improves error messages for unsupported chips.
The commit removes redundant software enable count management for PRCMU clocks in the ux500 platform, as the core already handles this state.
This commit refactors RISC-V memory management initialization by reducing #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage. It groups related conditional usages together for cleaner code structure.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to support future I2C nodes.
The commit fixes comments in the thermal driver for rzg2l by improving clarity and correcting typos.
Fixes an infinite loop issue when failing to acquire the HW semaphore during resource release, typically due to hardware removal.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI format terminology, replacing old-style defines with current terms for clocking.
The commit removes unnecessary code related to IS_SWAPFILE in NFS, improving code quality by eliminating redundancy.
Implements handling of GTP Echo Request messages by sending appropriate responses with necessary information elements for both GTPv0 and GTPv1.
The commit replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* where appropriate for better effectiveness. It also introduces a new function cpumask_first_zero() since it didn't exist before.
Removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR because the firmware no longer supports it, preventing failure and console errors during roaming.
Adds support for Intel platforms using the alc5682 codec without a speaker amplifier by creating a new board configuration.
The commit ensures that zerocopy sendmsg operations fail when the socket does not support them, preventing incorrect behavior.
The commit modifies the kerneldoc-preamble to test for the existence of xeCJK.sty before loading it, addressing issues where the file might be missing on certain distributions.
Modifies checkpatch.pl to allow 'case' macros without reporting errors, improving the tool's accuracy for existing code.
The commit replaces simple-KMS helpers with atomic helpers in the DRM subsystem to improve code architecture and facilitate easier sharing among drivers without introducing functional changes.
Adds a JPEG decode worker interface to enable parallel processing across three hardware units on MT8195.
The commit removes VPRINTK() calls and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() function in libata, as their information is already covered by tracepoints.

This commit adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank periods in FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO. It calculates the gained time from extra vblank lines and allows zstate entry if it exceeds 5 ms, optimizing min_dst_y_next_start.
Fixes a type mismatch in the hqd_destroy() callback to resolve a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) violation when running on specific hardware with CONFIG_CFI_CLANG enabled.
Corrects a typo in device tree bindings for STM32 pinctrl, changing 'reset' to 'resets', and adds schema validation to prevent future issues.

The commit addresses an array-bounds warning in the ath9k driver caused by GCC 12. It silences the warning by adjusting its priority, indicating that the issue is not critical but requires attention to prevent future bugs.
The commit replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get to simplify the code.

The commit reorganizes fixed file installation and removal functions into the filetable code, improving code structure. It modifies helper functions to use ctx instead of io_kiocb for better context handling.
The commit splits the G1 and G2 decoder blocks in the media device tree bindings for better independence and management under their respective power domains.
The commit removes an unused macro and combines its contents into another macro, indicating a refactoring effort to simplify the codebase.
